concepts.title: Role of Judiciary in Balancing Power
concepts.description: The Judiciary plays a crucial role in balancing power by declaring laws or actions of the executive or legislature as unconstitutional, thereby protecting the rights of citizens and maintaining the rule of law.

concepts.title: Concept of Alternative Centres of Power
concepts.description: According to NCERT, alternative centres of power refer to institutions, processes, and structures that can limit or counterbalance the power of the central government, such as the judiciary, the media, and civil society.

concepts.title: Supremacy of the Constitution
concepts.description: The Constitution is the supreme law of the land, and all other laws and institutions must conform to it. This principle is essential in maintaining the balance of power and upholding the rule of law in a democratic system.

concepts.title: Judicial Activism
concepts.description: Judicial activism can be remembered using the acronym 'J.A.C.K.S': Just, Assertive, Courageous, Knowledgeable, and Strong. This acronym helps students recall the key characteristics of judicial activism and its role in promoting justice and protecting rights.

concepts.title: Confusing Judicial Activism with Judicial Overreach
concepts.description: Students often confuse judicial activism with judicial overreach. Judicial activism refers to the judiciary's role in promoting justice and protecting rights, while judicial overreach refers to the judiciary exceeding its constitutional powers. Students must be able to distinguish between these two concepts.

concepts.title: Key Features of the Indian Constitution
concepts.description: Students must remember the key features of the Indian Constitution, including the supremacy of the Constitution, the rule of law, the separation of powers, and the protection of fundamental rights. These features are essential in understanding the balance of power in India.

concepts.title: Role of Civil Society in Balancing Power
concepts.description: Civil society plays a crucial role in balancing power by holding the government accountable for its actions, promoting transparency and good governance, and providing a platform for citizens to express their opinions and demands.
